---
title: Bring your own outputs
---

This tutorial walks the adoption wedge of `heormodel` end to end: a costs/effects PSA table produced by any external model is loaded with `as_outcomes` and driven through CEA, VoI, plots, and a run report, without touching a model engine. It is the narrated version of [`examples/byoo_example.py`](https://github.com/pedroliman/heormodel/blob/main/examples/byoo_example.py). It assumes you have [installed `heormodel`](../get-started.qmd).

## An external PSA table

Three strategies for a chronic disease: standard of care, a new drug, and drug plus monitoring. To stand in for a spreadsheet export or a legacy simulator, we synthesise the table from sampled parameters, so the VoI results later are traceable to known drivers.

```{python}
import numpy as np
import pandas as pd
from heormodel.params import Beta, Gamma, Normal, ParameterSet
from heormodel.run import SeedManager, as_outcomes

WTP = 30_000.0  # sits between the two frontier ICERs, so the decision is uncertain
N = 2_000

seed_manager = SeedManager(20260704)
params = ParameterSet(
    {
        "p_response": Beta.from_mean_se(0.35, 0.05),
        "rr_drug": Normal(0.75, 0.08),
        "c_drug": Gamma.from_mean_se(12_000, 1_500),
        "c_monitoring": Gamma.from_mean_se(2_000, 400),
        "u_gain": Beta.from_mean_se(0.12, 0.03),
    },
    correlation={("p_response", "u_gain"): 0.3},
)
draws = params.sample(N, seed=seed_manager.generator())

base_qaly = 8.0
effect_drug = base_qaly + draws["u_gain"] * draws["p_response"] / draws["rr_drug"] * 10
rows = pd.concat([
    pd.DataFrame({"strategy": "Standard care", "iteration": draws.index,
                  "cost": 40_000.0, "qaly": base_qaly}),
    pd.DataFrame({"strategy": "New drug", "iteration": draws.index,
                  "cost": 40_000.0 + draws["c_drug"], "qaly": effect_drug}),
    pd.DataFrame({"strategy": "Drug + monitoring", "iteration": draws.index,
                  "cost": 40_000.0 + draws["c_drug"] + draws["c_monitoring"],
                  "qaly": effect_drug + 0.15 * draws["p_response"]}),
])
```

## One call into the standard schema

`as_outcomes` accepts a tidy DataFrame or a CSV path and returns `Outcomes`, the schema every analysis consumes.

```{python}
outcomes = as_outcomes(rows)
outcomes.summary().round(2)
```

## Cost-effectiveness analysis

```{python}
from heormodel.cea import ceac, ceaf, icer_table

icer_table(outcomes).round(3)
```

The table orders strategies by cost and reports incremental results along the efficiency frontier. The `status` column marks dominated (`D`) and extended-dominated (`ED`) strategies; here all three are non-dominated (`ND`), and the willingness-to-pay threshold of 30,000 sits between the two frontier ICERs, so the decision is genuinely uncertain.

```{python}
grid = np.linspace(0, 80_000, 51)
ceac_df = ceac(outcomes, grid)
ceaf_df = ceaf(outcomes, grid)
```

## Value of information

EVPI at the willingness-to-pay threshold, then a single-parameter EVPPI ranking for research prioritisation. The ranking needs the draw matrix, linked to outcomes by the shared iteration index.

```{python}
from heormodel.voi import evpi, evppi_ranking

print(f"EVPI at WTP {WTP:,.0f}: {evpi(outcomes, WTP):,.1f}")
evppi_ranking(outcomes, draws, WTP).round(1)
```

## Plots

```{python}
from heormodel.report import plot_ce_plane, plot_ceac, plot_frontier, plot_tornado, tornado_data

plot_ce_plane(outcomes, comparator="Standard care", wtp=WTP);
```

```{python}
plot_ceac(ceac_df, ceaf_df=ceaf_df);
```

```{python}
td = tornado_data(outcomes, draws, WTP, strategy="New drug", comparator="Standard care")
plot_tornado(td);
```

## Reproducibility record

`capture_run` snapshots the seed, parameter specs, and outcome shape; the record serialises to JSON and renders a run report.

```{python}
from heormodel.report import capture_run

record = capture_run(seed=seed_manager, params=params, outcomes=outcomes,
                     note="Bring-your-own-outputs tutorial.")
print(record.to_markdown("heormodel example run report"))
```

Next: the [Markov cohort model](mdm-cohort.qmd) builds the standard state-transition model and runs its PSA through the same analysis layer.
